metadata.title: Agent Dashboard
metadata.description: Task-centric AI control plane for human and runtime collaboration.

taskCenterTable.title: Title
taskCenterTable.planningHint: Open task for planning details, or jump straight into work.

taskPage.fallbackDescription: Use this page for reference, deep links, and heavier context that would clutter Schedule.
taskPage.primarySurfacesDescription: Keep core planning and task configuration in Schedule, then move into Work for execution and collaboration.
taskPage.runtimeConfigurationDescription: Keep the runnable definition visible here, but treat Schedule as the default place to edit it.
taskPage.noPrompt: No prompt saved yet. Configure one in Schedule before execution.
taskPage.planningContextDescription: Keep the current plan visible without turning this page back into the main planning surface.
taskPage.dependenciesDescription: Track upstream work without pulling planning controls back into this page.

scheduleTaskList.title: Schedule List View
scheduleTaskList.description: The task-center successor: the same planning system, sliced for triage without leaving Schedule.
scheduleTaskList.showingSuffix: tasks. Use quick edit to fix priority, due date, and runnable config without bouncing through Task.
scheduleTaskList.emptyAll: No tasks available in Schedule yet.
scheduleTaskList.emptyWaitingForApproval: No tasks are waiting for approval right now.
scheduleTaskList.emptyUnscheduled: No tasks are waiting for placement.
scheduleTaskList.emptyOverdue: No overdue tasks are on the board.
scheduleTaskList.emptyNotRunnable: Every task in the current set is runnable.

workspaceOverview.sectionDescription: Keep the highest-value tasks visible without opening every detail page.

taskConfigForm.title: Title
taskConfigForm.titlePlaceholder: Add the next task to execute
taskConfigForm.promptPlaceholder: Describe the task, constraints, and expected outcome
taskConfigForm.descriptionPlaceholder: Optional execution context or desired outcome
taskConfigForm.errorInvalidJson: Runtime params must be valid JSON
taskConfigForm.errorJsonObject: Runtime params must be a JSON object

scheduleEditorForm.fieldRequired: At least one scheduling field is required.

schedulePage.clickOrDrag: Click any slot or drag to adjust
schedulePage.emptyDayLaneDescription: Drop a queued task anywhere on this lane to create the first block.
schedulePage.taskDetailsDescription: Review the selected block in a floating panel, then return to the timeline.
schedulePage.dragHint: Drag to the timeline or expand for details and fallback scheduling.
schedulePage.scheduledMetricHint: Committed blocks on the current plan.
schedulePage.queueMetricHint: Tasks still waiting to enter the timeline.
schedulePage.aiProposalsMetricHint: Pending suggestions that need a decision.
schedulePage.scheduledTimelineDescription: This is the main planning surface: create tasks, place queued work, and drag existing blocks to adjust time.
schedulePage.conflictsDescription: Keep recovery work visible without crowding the queue rail.
schedulePage.noScheduleRisks: No schedule risks detected. Blocked, overdue, or interrupted work will surface here.
schedulePage.aiProposalsDescription: Review suggestions near the timeline, not inside the queue rail.
schedulePage.noAiProposals: No pending AI proposals. When planner automation suggests a new block, it will appear here for review.
schedulePage.guideStep2: 2. Drag queue items into concrete blocks or click a free slot to create work inline.
schedulePage.guideStep3: 3. Review proposals as suggestions, not automatic truth.
schedulePage.noTimelineDay: No timeline day is available right now.
schedulePage.unscheduledQueueDescription: Collapsed task cards stay in the side rail. Expand only when needed, or drag them directly into the timeline.
schedulePage.noUnscheduledWork: No unscheduled work. New tasks that lose their plan or need initial placement will appear here.
schedulePage.todayFocusEmpty: No blocking items to handle today. You can pull the next time block from the unscheduled queue.

taskAiSidebar.cockpitDescription: Replace the original subtask area with the graph from AI Task Plan.
taskAiSidebar.planAcceptedAndApplied: Task plan accepted and applied. New subtasks have been created.
taskAiSidebar.decompositionFailed: Failed to apply task decomposition. Please try again.
taskAiSidebar.savedPlanTitle: Current saved plan title and main flow.
taskAiSidebar.aiCockpitHeading: Let AI produce an actionable task plan
taskAiSidebar.aiCockpitDescription: Auto-generates on first visit when no plan exists; afterwards shows the saved plan and lets you re-plan with a custom prompt.
taskAiSidebar.aiPlanningDescription: Auto-saves unaccepted plans; after acceptance keeps the current version and lets you re-plan.
taskAiSidebar.planningPromptPlaceholder: e.g.: Prioritize regulatory checks, then schedule timeline; or break down to fit in one day
taskAiSidebar.acceptedNoAutoRerun: Accepted — will not auto-rerun unless you re-plan

workPage.workbenchDescription: Add constraints, change direction, specify output format, or provide missing context.
workPage.taskArrangementHint: E.g.: First clarify the task objective, list constraints, then give me a brief execution plan in bullet points.
workPage.continueRunDescription: This run has finished. Review the latest result first, then continue the next run directly from here instead of switching to a new flow.
workPage.followUpOptionalDescription: Only create a follow-up task when the work truly needs to split into a new track.
workPage.fallbackNoOperatorInput: No task records yet. After starting, key milestones such as execution start, waiting for confirmation, or output updates will appear here in chronological order.
workPage.fallbackStartFromTaskPage: Start a run first, then add requirements from here.
workPage.sharedOutputDescription: Keep the latest available result near the work area so you don't have to scroll through logs.
workPage.executionWorkstreamDescription: Background records are collapsed here by default; switch to conversation view when you need to trace details.
workPage.taskCockpitDescription: Pin current blockers, suggested actions, recent output, and risks in the sidebar to avoid scrolling through the execution stream.
workPage.executionSnapshotDescription: Pin key information needed to assess task status in the sidebar, separate from the event stream.
workPage.noSuggestedAction: No extra actions right now, continue observing execution.
workPage.conversationEvidenceDescription: Expand the full conversation when milestone summaries aren't enough.
workPage.noteQueuedForCheckpoint: Message will be delivered at the next safe checkpoint and displayed after sync.
workPage.responseRequiredDescription: The blocked run can only continue after you provide additional input.
workPage.noteWhileRunningDescription: You can add context while the run is in progress, but it won't forcefully interrupt the current execution.
workPage.noteWhileAwaitingApprovalDescription: You can add context before approval is complete, but the run will still wait for the approval result.
workPage.currentRunCannotAcceptMessages: The current run cannot accept operator messages right now.
workPage.keyboardHint: Enter to submit · Shift+Enter for new line
workPage.taskReadySummary: Task has not started yet. The most important decision now is whether to start the first run from this workbench.
workPage.taskRunningSummary: Task is running. Only intervene when you need to correct course or add constraints.
workPage.taskWaitingInputSummary: Agent is waiting for your additional input before continuing.
workPage.taskWaitingApprovalSummary: Agent is waiting for your decision; approval is needed before continuing.
workPage.taskCompletedSummary: The latest round is complete. Review the result first, then decide whether to continue.
workPage.taskFailedSummary: The last run was interrupted. Decide now whether to recover, retry, or change direction.
workPage.collaborationFlowDescription: After input, messages appear in chronological order below: Agent on the left, you on the right.
workPage.resultEmptyDescription: After starting, this area will show the Agent's first-round understanding, execution suggestions, drafts, or questions for your confirmation.
workPage.resultPreviewTitle: After starting, this area typically shows:
workPage.milestoneWaitingStart: Waiting for your decision to start
workPage.planReadySummary: The plan updates with the current run status.
workPage.planEmptySummary: After acceptance, the plan graph will show the current and next nodes here.
workPage.approvalSummaryFallback: Please review this approval before the run can continue.
workPage.quickPromptRetryC: Narrow the scope of this change
workPage.currentRunNotWaitingForInput: The current run is not waiting for direct operator input.
workPage.startRunDescription: Start the first run here so the workbench becomes the live execution surface instead of a dead end.
workPage.resultActionsDescription: Keep these controls secondary. You can continue the work above at any time.
workPage.followUpOptionalDescription_alt: Only create a follow-up task when the work truly needs to split into a new track.

runSidePanel.runSnapshotDescription: Keep this side rail as a lightweight inspector for run health, approvals, artifacts, and tool evidence.
runSidePanel.evidenceDescription: Approvals, artifacts, and tool output stay here as supporting context rather than the main work area.
runSidePanel.blockerSummary: No clear blocker, task can proceed.

executionTimeline.title: Execution Timeline
executionTimeline.backgroundHint: These records are mainly for tracing context. They are collapsed by default to avoid overwhelming the truly critical milestones.

workPage.humanApprovalMatters: Human approval or review directly affects whether this run can continue.
workPage.noPlannedWindowYet: No planned window exists yet. Place or adjust the task from Schedule.
workPage.executionTimingSlipping: Execution timing is slipping against the planned window.
workPage.overdueRecovery: The task is beyond its expected window and needs recovery.
workPage.scheduleAligned: Schedule remains aligned with the current plan.
workPage.noRunActiveDescription: No run is active yet. Launch one from this workbench once the task is ready in Schedule.
workPage.noActiveRunWhy: There is no active run, so execution cannot progress from this page yet.
workPage.waitingForGuidance: The agent is waiting for operator guidance.
workPage.pausedUntilReply: The run is paused until the operator replies.
workPage.blockedOnApproval: The run is blocked on an approval decision before it can continue.
workPage.humanDecisionRequired: A human decision is required before the next execution step can proceed.
workPage.stoppedBeforeFinishing: The last run stopped before finishing. Retry with a focused recovery prompt.
workPage.executionStopped: Execution stopped and will not progress until a recovery action is taken.
workPage.completedDescription: The run completed. Review the latest result and continue directly from the workbench when you are ready.
workPage.resultAvailableWhy: The latest result is available. The key decision now is how to keep the work moving, not how to step through a complex closing flow.
workPage.runActiveDescription: The run is still active. Watch the newest milestones and intervene only if the state changes.
workPage.agentExecutingWhy: The agent is currently executing, so the best next step is to monitor the newest evidence.
workPage.inspectBeforeActing: Review the latest output and inspector state before acting.
workPage.stateNeedsInspection: The run state needs inspection before the next action is clear.
workPage.latestArtifactAppears: The latest artifact or agent result will appear here first.

tasks.title: Task Center
tasks.subtitle: Focus on running, blocked, approval-waiting, unscheduled, and overdue work without reading full transcripts.
tasks.createDescription: Capture a new unit of work, then open it immediately for planning or execution.
tasks.titlePlaceholder: Add the next task to execute
tasks.descriptionPlaceholder: Optional execution context, desired outcome, or constraints

inbox.title: Inbox
inbox.subtitle: Triage approvals, input requests, schedule proposals, and recovery work from one interruption queue.

memory.title: Memory
memory.subtitle: Review active memory records and deactivate stale guidance when it no longer applies.

workspaces.title: Workspaces
workspaces.subtitle: Advanced workspace management for internal or multi-workspace operations.
workspaces.notice: Daily work now starts from Schedule, Tasks, Inbox, and Memory. This page remains available for deeper workspace administration.

settings.title: Settings
settings.subtitle: Runtime connection details for the current control-plane environment.
settings.advancedDescription: Workspace management and deeper operational controls live outside the daily task flow.
settings.aiClientsDescription: Manage AI clients and configure which client each feature uses

aiClientsPage.title: AI Clients
aiClientsPage.subtitle: Manage AI clients and configure which client each feature uses
aiClientsPage.emptyState: No AI Clients configured yet. Click the button above to add one.

advancedSettings.title: Advanced Settings
advancedSettings.subtitle: Internal controls that stay available without becoming the default workflow.
advancedSettings.workspaceManagementDescription: The product now defaults into a single-workspace UX. Use workspace management only for advanced or internal operations.

workspaceOverview.title: Workspace Overview
workspaceOverview.subtitle: Triage runs, approvals, and schedule risks before diving into execution detail.
